application_based_attack_scenarios.description: Injection of malicious client-side scripts into vulnerable web application inputs.
attack_steps: Inject malicious JavaScript payload into a user input field
Application reflects or stores the payload without proper sanitization
Script executes in the victim's browser when the page is loaded

application_based_attack_scenarios.description: Execution of arbitrary operating system commands due to improper input validation.
attack_steps: Submit crafted input containing OS command separators
Application concatenates input into a system-level command
Injected command executes on the server

application_based_attack_scenarios.description: Unauthorized access to restricted files by manipulating file path parameters.
attack_steps: Manipulate file path using traversal sequences
Access sensitive system or application files

application_based_attack_scenarios.description: Upload and execution of malicious files due to weak validation controls.
attack_steps: Upload a malicious file disguised as a legitimate file type
Server stores the file without enforcing validation rules

application_based_attack_scenarios.description: Forcing authenticated users to perform unintended actions without their consent.
attack_steps: Browser submits forged request using valid session

application_based_attack_scenarios.description: Exploitation of weak authentication and session management mechanisms.
attack_steps: Perform credential stuffing or brute-force attacks
Gain unauthorized access to user accounts
blue_team_evaluation_metrics: Session invalidation and access revocation time
